2605.12436.title: CAAFC: Chronological Actionable Automated Fact-Checker for misinformation / non-factual hallucination detection and correction
2605.12436.paper_summary: With the vast amount of content uploaded every hour, along with the AI generated content that can include hallucinations, Automated Fact-Checking (AFC) has become increasingly vital, as it is infeasible for human fact-checkers to manually verify the sheer volume of information generated online. Professional fact-checkers have identified several gaps in existing AFC systems, noting a misalignment between how these systems operate and how fact-checking is performed in practice. In this paper, we introduce CAAFC (Chronological Actionable Automated Fact-Checker), a frame-work designed to bridge these gaps. It surpasses SOTA AFC and hallucination detection systems across multiple benchmark datasets. CAAFC operates on claims, conversations, and dialogues, enabling it not only to detect factual errors and hallucinations, but also to correct them by providing actionable justifications supported by primary information sources. Furthermore, CAAFC can update evidence and knowledge bases by incorporating recent and contextual information when necessary, thereby enhancing the reliability of fact verification.
2605.12436.paper_summary_zh: 隨著每小時上傳的大量內容，以及可能包含幻覺的AI生成內容，自動化事實核查（AFC）變得越來越重要，因為人類事實核查員無法手動驗證在線生成的龐大信息量。專業的事實核查員已經識別出現有AFC系統中的幾個缺口，指出這些系統的運作方式與實際進行事實核查的方式之間存在不一致。在本文中，我們介紹CAAFC（時間序列可行自動事實核查器），這是一個旨在彌補這些缺口的框架。它在多個基準數據集上超越了最先進的AFC和幻覺檢測系統。CAAFC針對聲明、對話和交流進行操作，使其不僅能檢測事實錯誤和幻覺，還能通過提供由主要信息來源支持的可行理由來糾正這些錯誤。此外，CAAFC還可以在必要時通過納入最新和上下文信息來更新證據和知識庫，從而提高事實核查的可靠性。
2605.12436.author: Islam Eldifrawi et.al.
2605.12436.authors: Islam Eldifrawi, Shengrui Wang, Amine Trabelsi
